nameMeanings.name: Dollns
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Somerset): variant of Dollin, with post-medieval excrescent -s, itself a variant of Dolling, a nickname from an unrecorded Middle English dolling, douling, dulling ‘dull or stupid one’ (compare Doll ). The name may also have arisen from a late assimilation of rl to ll in Dorling, a variant of Darling . Compare Dollens .

variants.name: Dollison
meanings: English: variant of Dollinson (see Dollins ).

variants.name: Dowland
meanings: English (Dorset): habitational name from Dowland in Devon, possibly named from Old English dūfe ‘dove’ + land ‘open country’. The name is pronounced with the first element rhyming with owl, but it may have been confused with and absorbed by Dolling, also a Devon name (see Dollins ). The name may also be a post-medieval variant of Dolling (see Dollins ), with shortening of -ing to /ən/, as in Dowlen , to which excrescent -d was added at will.
Irish: probably a variant of   Dowlin   or   Dolan  .

variants.name: Dollens
meanings: Altered form of English   Dollins  .

variants.name: Doll
meanings: South German: nickname from Middle High German tol, dol ‘foolish, mad’; also ‘strong, handsome’.
South German (Döll): variant of Thiel .
South German (Bavaria): topographic name for someone living in a valley, Middle High German tol ‘ditch’.

nameMeanings.name: Gebhard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from geb, gib ‘gift’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. St Gebhard was a 10th-century bishop of Constance who founded the abbey of Petershausen.

variants.name: Gebbert
meanings: from geb, gib ‘gift’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. St Gebhard was a 10th-century bishop of Constance who founded the abbey of Petershausen.

nameMeanings.name: Von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant of   Ivo  , derived from the oblique case of the name.

nameMeanings.name: Hirschberg
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hanns, Manfred, Erwin, Gunter, Klaus, Raimund.  Jewish Aharon, Emanuel, Naftoli, Noson, Yerachmiel.

German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name from any of several places called Hirschberg, from Middle High German hirz ‘deer, stag’ + berg ‘mountain, hill’, for example in Thuringia, North Rhine-Westphalia, or in western Poland (Jelenia Góra). In some instances the German surname may be topographic, from the same elements.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name composed of German Hirsch ‘deer’ + Berg ‘mountain, hill’ (see 1 above). Compare Hershberg and Hirshberg .

variants.name: Hershberg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Hyman, Yehoshua.
Americanized form of German Herschberg (see Herschberger ) or Hirschberg , and a variant of the same Jewish surnames.

variants.name: Hirshberg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Isadore.
Americanized form of German   Hirschberg  , and a variant of the same Jewish surname.

variants.name: Hirschberger
meanings: German: habitational name for someone from any of several places called   Hirschberg  .
German: altered form of Swiss German   Hersberger  . Compare   Harshbarger  .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): mainly an artificial name composed of German Hirsch ‘deer, hart’ + Berg ‘mountain, hill’ + the agent suffix -er, but possibly also habitational in some cases (see 1 above).

en.lifeSummary: When Gebhard Von Grögling Hirschberg Dollns was born in 1160, in Germany, his father, Gerhard I von Hirschberg, was -3 and his mother, Sophie von Sulzbach, was 22. He had at least 1 son with Agnes Von Truhendingen. He died in 1232, in his hometown, at the age of 72.
